typography_rules: Use one native editable headline as the first attention target.
Use a deterministic hierarchy ladder for headline, proof label, note, and trace caption.
Keep launch copy short enough to read from the contact sheet.
spacing_rules: Pair the largest type role with a visibly larger whitespace field.
Keep trace captions separated from the primary launch claim.
composition_rules: Do not let setup or proof become equal-weight text panels.
Anchor each slide with one dominant native object or headline zone.
rhythm_rules: Typography scale should rise from setup into proof and simplify again on close.
native_ppt_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT text boxes for every title and label.
Trace the selected typography memory id in each generated slide.
qa_probes: The contact sheet should show one clear typographic hierarchy per slide.

typography_rules: Use spacing to make headline, proof object, and trace note read as distinct levels.
spacing_rules: Use large outer margins, visible gutters, and one dominant proof zone.
Increase whitespace before the climax so scale change is visible.
composition_rules: Keep proof grids editorial, asymmetric, and visibly prioritized.
Avoid equal card rows unless one proof object is clearly dominant.
rhythm_rules: Use tighter proof spacing after setup, then open the climax canvas.
native_ppt_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT coordinates, shape groups, and text boxes.
Trace spacing memory ids and geometry decisions for QA.
qa_probes: The contact sheet should show visible margins, gutters, and a dominant proof zone.

typography_rules: Use one short native label near the climax object and route explanation away from the canvas.
spacing_rules: Leave enough whitespace around the climax object for its scale to be visible.
composition_rules: Allocate 40-55% of the canvas to one native proof object.
The one native proof object must be original, editable, and visually stronger than all supporting labels.
rhythm_rules: The climax is the largest visual beat after proof, not another setup or grid slide.
native_ppt_generation_requirements: Generate the proof object with native editable PPT shapes, connectors, and labels.
Trace the composition memory id, source record ids, and quality gate.
qa_probes: The contact sheet should show the climax object as the largest supported visual decision.

typography_rules: Let typography density rise through proof and simplify at the close.
spacing_rules: Use rhythm-aware whitespace: sparse cover, structured setup, tighter proof, open climax, simple close.
composition_rules: Each rhythm role needs a distinct native composition, not repeated report panels.
rhythm_rules: Preserve cover, setup, contrast, proof, climax, close in that order.
Do not add a Run 3 stage or extra relief slide for Run 2.7.
native_ppt_generation_requirements: Generate each rhythm role with native editable PPT objects.
Trace rhythm memory ids and slide role decisions in the manifest.
qa_probes: The contact sheet should make the six-slide rhythm visible without reading body text.

typography_rules: Use original wording and neutral product-system labels instead of source phrasing.
spacing_rules: Change source-like spacing patterns into an original editorial layout before generation.
composition_rules: Represent product surfaces as original native abstractions, not copied UI frames.
rhythm_rules: Source sanitization applies before every slide role is generated.
native_ppt_generation_requirements: Use native editable PPT objects with trace fields proving source-brand sanitization.
Record source record ids and design memory ids for each slide.
qa_probes: The contact sheet should show no source-brand resemblance while retaining derived design intent.
